content:
Chromium-based browsers on Linux (Chrome, Brave, etc.) don’t have a built-in UI language setting, but you can still change it manually.
1) One-time: CLI The simplest way is to pass the LANGUAGE environment variable when launching the browser.
🇺🇸 English (EN)
1 LANGUAGE=en_US:en chromium-browser 🇹🇷 Turkish (TR)
1 LANGUAGE=tr_TR:tr chromium-browser The command name may differ depending on your distro. Examples:
chromium-browser chromium or full path: /usr/bin/chromium-browser Generic format:
1 LANGUAGE=target_lang /path/to/browser Do you need LC_ALL? Usually no. If parts of the UI still show the old language/format, you can use a stronger override:
🇺🇸 English (EN)
1 LANGUAGE=en_US:en LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 chromium-browser 🇹🇷 Turkish (TR)
1 LANGUAGE=tr_TR:tr LC_ALL=tr_TR.UTF-8 chromium-browser 2) Permanent: GUI To make it permanent, add env LANGUAGE=... at the start of the Exec= line in the app’s .desktop launcher.
Instead of editing the system .desktop file directly, the cleanest approach is:
Find the .desktop file (usually under /usr/share/applications/). Copy it to ~/.local/share/applications/. Edit the copied file’s Exec= line. Example transformation:
Before: Exec=chromium-browser %U
After (🇺🇸 EN): Exec=env LANGUAGE=en_US:en chromium-browser %U
After (🇹🇷 TR): Exec=env LANGUAGE=tr_TR:tr chromium-browser %U
3) Flatpak One-time 🇺🇸 English (EN)
1 flatpak run --env=LANGUAGE=en_US:en org.chromium.Chromium 🇹🇷 Turkish (TR)
1 flatpak run --env=LANGUAGE=tr_TR:tr org.chromium.Chromium Permanent 🇺🇸 English (EN)
1 flatpak override --user --env=LANGUAGE=en_US:en org.chromium.Chromium 🇹🇷 Turkish (TR)
1 flatpak override --user --env=LANGUAGE=tr_TR:tr org.chromium.Chromium To revert:
1 flatpak override --user --unset-env=LANGUAGE org.chromium.Chromium Tip: Run locale -a to list the available languages on your system. To browse the exact language codes, check the List of ISO 639 language codes.
summary:
Chromium-based browsers on Linux (Chrome, Brave, etc.) don’t have a built-in UI language setting, but you can still change it manually.

1) One-time: CLI

The simplest way is to pass the LANGUAGE environment variable when launching the browser.

🇺🇸 English (EN)

1

LANGUAGE=en_US:en chromium-browser

🇹🇷 Turkish (TR)

1

LANGUAGE=tr_TR:tr chromium-browser

The command name may differ depending on your distro. Examples:

  • chromium-browser
  • chromium
  • or full path: /usr/bin/chromium-browser

Generic format:
title: Change Chromium-Based Browser Language on Linux

content:
Privacy furuy.com is a personal blog. No ads or tracking tools are used.
What data may be processed? Server logs: The site is hosted on GitHub Pages. The hosting/CDN layer may generate technical logs (IP address, request time, requested URL, user agent, etc.) for security and performance purposes. Third-party services Comments (Giscus / GitHub Discussions): Comments are provided via Giscus and backed by GitHub Discussions. Loading the comments section sends requests to Giscus and GitHub infrastructure; posting may require a GitHub account and authorization. Giscus Privacy Policy GitHub Privacy Policies Cookies and similar technologies This website does not use advertising or tracking cookies.
Preferences (browser storage): User preferences (such as theme) may be stored in browser storage (e.g., localStorage key pref-theme). This information stays on your device and is not sent to the site server. Comments: Loading comments may involve cookies or similar storage mechanisms from Giscus/GitHub. License (CC0 1.0) Unless otherwise stated, the content on this website is dedicated to the public domain under the CC0 1.0 Universal license.
In summary:
You can copy, modify, share, and use the work commercially. No permission is required. Attribution is not required (though appreciated). Creative Commons CC0 1.0 Deed
summary: Privacy policy, cookie usage, and license information.
title: Legal
